polygon.data-contents: The road toward Lake Aurenmere and Aurenholt. Better kept on the Aurenne side; pilgrims and knights pass through.
Deep in Tumblewood, the cell of Brother Ossian, an exiled Aurennois priest who heals those who find him and asks hard questions.
The seat of Baron Cael Thorn, nominal lord of Halvedd: a squat border-keep, a thin garrison, and a lord who owes money to Valmoria he cannot pay.
A lone hill crowned by a long-empty gibbet, the march's old boundary-mark. You can see the whole valley from the top.
A tangled second-growth forest reclaiming abandoned imperial farmland. Charcoalers, poachers, and a band of broken-men outlaws under one 'Captain Crow.'
A farming hamlet on the King's Road west toward Aurenne. Sheep, a shrine, and a miller who hears everything.
A pre-Sundering noble's villa swallowed by Duskmoor; its tiled halls are below the waterline now, and not empty.
A grey, ash-choked no-man's-land where Halvedd ends and the Sundering Scar begins. Nothing grows; the air tastes of old lightning.
A reeking fen where the Silverflow spreads and slows. Will-o'-wisps, a sunken causeway, and stories of something that takes the unwary at dusk.
The walled caravan-town at the march's heart and the party's home base: market, the Broken Wheel inn, a chapter-house of the Free Companies, and Reeve Maddox who keeps the peace badly.
The march's best farmland, worked in common since the old manor burned. The harvest here is what everyone north of the river is quietly counting on.
A plain of fused black glass where an imperial legion died in the Sundering. Treasure-hunters dig here; few dig twice.
The shallow crossing of the Silverflow on the King's Road. A toll-post, a ferryman, and the wreck of the old stone bridge the Sundering shook down.
The great river that runs from Aurenne to Sahl, here broad and slow. Barge-traffic, fishing-clans, and good fast travel for those with a boat.
A roadside shrine and waystation tended by lay-sisters of the Dawnmother. Safe beds, weak ale, and the only consecrated ground for a day's ride.
A half-collapsed pre-Sundering mage-tower tilting over the Ashmarch. Its lower vaults are intact, warded, and unlooted. A classic first delve.
A poor hamlet on the Valmoria road, half its folk refugees from the Bonelands. Resentful, hungry, and watched by Valmorian tax-agents.
A warren in a quarried hillside where a goblin tribe -- pushed south out of the Bonelands -- now raids the Valmoria road.
A ring of imperial boundary-stones on the Ashmarch's edge that murmur in a dead tongue when the wild magic rises. Wizards pay for rubbings; few will camp here.
Rolling downs studded with the green mounds of a people older than the Imperium. Shepherds avoid them after dark; the barrows are not all sealed.
The rutted imperial road leaving the march toward Vael's Crossing and Valmoria. Merchant trains, and the bandits who love them.
The greatest of the downs' tombs, its entry-stone cracked open last winter. Cold air and a faint keening come out of it. Nothing has come back in.
A surviving imperial bridge over a Silverflow tributary, held by a self-appointed 'baron' charging passage east. The Baron of Thornhold disputes the claim, weakly.
A Free-Company strongpoint abandoned after its captain's company was wiped out in the Bonelands. The Compact will pay to see it reclaimed.
Where Halvedd frays into the lawless Bonelands. Warlord outriders, refugees, and the dust of someone's recent war.

svg.text: The frontier march of Halvedd — the party's home ground, between Aurenne, Valmoria, the Free Companies, and the Scar's edge.
